title: AD Escalation — Pick the Vector from Your Finding
body: In a domain, credential reuse beats exploits. You rarely need a CVE — you need to find who's roastable, who's logged in where, and whose creds you can replay. Match the finding to the vector.
cmd:
# AD ESCALATION - match FINDING → VECTOR. Creds (memory/disk) > exploits.

# FINDING: user has an SPN  (BloodHound "kerberoastable" / GetUserSPNs)
#   → KERBEROAST: request TGS, crack offline. Quiet, no host touch.
#   BEYOND: daniela had http/internalsrv1 SPN → roast → cracked.

# FINDING: user has "Do not require preauth"
#   → AS-REP ROAST: pull hash unauthenticated, crack offline.

# FINDING: SMB signing NOT required + you can coerce auth
#   → NTLM RELAY: relay to a target, run a payload.
#   BEYOND: relayed to INTERNALSRV1.

# FINDING: local admin on a box where a HIGHER-PRIV user has a session
#   → DUMP LSASS (mimikatz / sa): cleartext + NTLM in memory.
#   BEYOND: beccy (DA) had a session on the relayed host → mimikatz.

# FINDING: BloodHound shows an ACL edge (GenericAll/WriteDACL) you OWN
#   → ACL ABUSE: reset pw / add to group / RBCD.
#   (CONFIRM the edge in BloodHound - winPEAS ACL claims are often false.)

# FINDING: you hold DA / replication rights
#   → DCSYNC: pull krbtgt + all hashes. Domain owned.

# FINDING: a cracked/looted password
#   → SPRAY it across users/hosts (nxc) - reuse is the #1 AD win,
#   then PtH / psexec with the hash or password to land on the target.
warn: Confirm ACL findings (GenericAll/WriteDACL) in BloodHound before acting - local enum tools like winPEAS routinely over-report domain ACLs. The graph that resolves effective access is the arbiter, not the winPEAS dump.

choices.label: Admin on a box w/ a priv session — dump LSASS
